own_review.point: The change flips the 5th arg (optional) of MetadataFactory.createRequirement to true. Crucially, the 6-arg overload defaults greedy=true, so bundles that ARE resolvable are still pulled into the slice/target; only unresolvable ones (e.g. platform-filtered fragments like the SWT case) are skipped. This is the key verification that makes the one-character fix safe, and it is consistent end-to-end: OsgiBundleProject (~lines 405-418) already only warns ('Missing extra classpath entry') when the bundle is absent from the resolved artifacts.

Blanket behavior change: ALL platform-URL extra classpath requirements (both jars.extra.classpath and per-jar extra.<jar> maps, which funnel through the same createRequirementFromPlatformURL) become optional. Typos or genuinely missing required bundles no longer fail resolution; the error is deferred to a warning and possible later compile failure. Deliberate PDE-parity trade-off, but worth calling out as it can mask real configuration errors.

No test added for the new lenient behavior. tycho-its/src/test/java/org/eclipse/tycho/test/TYCHO253extraClassPathEntries/ExtraClassPathEntriesTest.java only covers successfully resolvable entries; an IT with an unresolvable platform-specific/fragment entry would prevent regression.

These advice-based extra requirements are created independently of Tycho's OptionalResolutionAction (REQUIRE/IGNORE/OPTIONAL) mechanism used in BundleDependenciesAction, so the new optionality is unconditional and not user-configurable.
Import reshuffle of StatusTool is unrelated cosmetic noise; harmless.

qualitative: All three candidates land at the same net because each contributes exactly one substantive verified point while missing the human reviewer's single — and decisive — observation: that MetadataFactory.createRequirement's 6-arg overload keeps greedy=true, which is what makes 'optional' safe (available bundles are still pulled in; only unresolvable ones are skipped). v2 is qualitatively strongest on design insight, being the only one to notice the requirement bypasses the OptionalResolutionAction mechanism and is thus unconditionally optional, with four accurately verified code citations. v4 is the most rigorously verified review overall (every cited file/line and IT build.properties claim checked out) and shares with v3 the useful missing-test recommendation, but pads with descriptive scope/pattern reassurance. v3 is the leanest, with a solid test-gap point and nothing wrong. Notably, no candidate fabricated anything — every concrete claim across v2/v3/v4 verified against the repo — but v3 and v4 both failed to flag the user-visible strictness regression (typos in jars.extra.classpath no longer fail the build), which v2 only obliquely captured via its configurability remark.
